title: FID: learning phase must USE failed experience — cross-cycle correction injection, anti-repetition guard, retro action vocabulary, lesson retrieval, advisor feedback
description:
USER DIRECTIVE (2026-07-02): 'is there a way to improve the learning phase to make use of the failed experience?' VERIFIED GAP: no phase prompt ever receives failure context (grep of phase_input_envelope/cyclerun_dispatch/prompts for failure/lesson injection = EMPTY); failedApproaches feeds ONLY the failure-adapter PROCEED/RETRY/BLOCK verdicts and the narrow ADR-0046 demotion. Result observed live: cycle 449 repeated cycle 448's triage-cap death IDENTICALLY (same gate, same template) — the record existed, the next cycle never read it. Every manual operator intervention that session is one missing mechanism.

DESIGN (L1-L5, TDD red-first, deterministic where possible — Rule 5):
L1 CROSS-CYCLE CORRECTION INJECTION (highest ROI, zero LLM cost): when the previous cycle under the SAME goal-hash failed at phase P with a gate rejection reason R, inject a '## Prior-cycle failure (same goal)' block into P's next prompt: R VERBATIM (never paraphrased) + the gate's counted specifics. This is the existing correction ladder extended across the cycle boundary. Golden: replay the 448->449 fixtures — the second cycle's triage prompt MUST contain the first's rejection text.
L2 ANTI-REPETITION GUARD: reuse ReasonTemplateHash (triagecap/demotion.go); if this cycle's deliverable draws the SAME rejection template that killed the previous cycle (same goal), do NOT burn the 2-correction ladder again — escalate immediately to retro-adapt (and count toward demotion). A failed approach may not be re-attempted verbatim.
L3 RETRO ACTION VOCABULARY: retro today emits prose + proceed/adapt. Give it a small ALLOWLISTED action set executed by the orchestrator at cycle end, each logged + bounded: (a) goal-append — append a constraint block to the goal card for subsequent cycles (max once per goal per failure-class; mechanizes the operator's cycle-450 pacing patch); (b) inbox-file — file a defect item (generalizes autoFileDemotionDefect); (c) gate-shadow — recommend one-cycle demotion of a named gate (START IN SHADOW STAGE: log-only until soaked; also heals the ADR-0046 reset-hole by considering the last two RECORDED same-template rejections within a 3-cycle window, treating reset-sealed cycles as transparent); (d) prompt-note — pin a lesson to phase P's prompt for N cycles under this goal.
L4 LESSON RETRIEVAL AT COMPOSE-TIME: failure-lesson YAMLs (retro already writes them) get {phase, gate, goal_hash} tags; every phase's ComposePrompt retrieves top-k matching lessons (scout + adversarial-review already grep the KB by convention — make it structural for all phases, bounded token budget).
L5 ADVISOR FEEDBACK: the advisor's plan prompt receives the failure summary (phase, gate, reason template) so it can re-route structure: insert phases (bug-reproduction archetype exists), reorder, narrow scope — learning expressed through the authority MR1-MR5 already gave it.

SAFETY RAILS: all retro actions allowlisted + logged + bounded; gate-shadow shadow-staged first; L1 injection verbatim-quoted; goal-append idempotent per failure-class; nothing weakens any gate's enforce semantics.

ACCEPTANCE: golden replay of the 448/449 fixtures (preserved run dirs; also seeded into RT-T1 testdata) proves (i) the second cycle's triage prompt contains the first's rejection verbatim, (ii) an identical-template second rejection escalates to adapt WITHOUT burning 2 corrections, (iii) a retro goal-append lands in the goal overlay and the next cycle's scout/triage read it; absent failures => byte-identical prompts (golden); go test -race + apicover -enforce clean. COORDINATE: RT campaign T1 (shares fixtures), triagecap-prose-counter-defect F2/F4 (actionable correctives + demotion window — do not duplicate), advisor-emit-tier-dormant (advisor prompt surface).
